sections.paragraph:
ἐναγκαλίζομαι 1 aor. ptc. ἐναγκαλισάμενος; pf. ptc. ἐνηγκαλισμένον (TestAbr B 2 p. 105, 10 [Stone p. 58]) (Meleager [I b.c.]: Anth. Pal. 7, 476, 10; Plut., Mor. 492d; Alciphron 4, 19, 5; IG XII/7, 395, 25 ὧν τέκνα ἐνηνκαλίσατο; Pr 6:10; 24:33; TestJob 52:10; JosAs 19:3; Mel., P. 24, 164 [B]) take in one’s arms, hug τινά someone Mk 9:36; 10:16 (Diod S 3, 58, 2f: Cybele takes little children into her arms [ἐναγκ.] and cures them [σῴζω] when they are sick; hence she is commonly called ‘mother of the mountain’ [ὀρεία μήτηρ]).—M-M. DELG s.v. ἀγκ-.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pf. = perfect

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 330
